items.summary: Sebi said restricting borrowing only to redemption payouts and guaranteed receivables could impair fund management flexibility and potentially hurt scheme returns.

items.summary: European stocks bucked the negative sentiment seen in Tuesday's session by opening higher on Wednesday.

items.summary: Britain faces among the highest borrowing costs of any developed nation — but bond markets offer 4 key lessons the government can embrace to remedy the issue.

items.title: India’s first indigenous 200-kmph Ahmedabad-Dholera rail corridor worth Rs 20,667 crore gets Cabinet nod
items.summary: A new semi-high-speed rail line will connect Ahmedabad and Dholera in Gujarat. The project, costing around Rs 20,667 crore, will be India's first with indigenous technology. It aims to reduce travel time significantly, boosting connectivity and regional development. The line will operate at 200 kmph.

items.title: Cabinet approves Rs 37,500 crore coal gasification scheme to cut import dependence
items.summary: The Union Cabinet on Wednesday approved a Rs 37,500 crore scheme to promote surface coal and lignite gasification projects, aiming to gasify around 75 million tonnes of coal and attract investments of up to Rs 3 lakh crore, Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said during a Cabinet briefing.

items.title: Beyond the ‘avocado consumer’: How Aarti Gill built a supplement powerhouse
items.summary: Oziva has gone from struggling to secure a ₹10 lakh bank loan to being acquired by one of India’s largest consumer goods companies. We retrace the rollercoaster journey Aarti Gill and Mihir Gadani endured to get from start to finish.

items.title: Honasa Consumer appoints two senior leaders to drive new brand building
items.summary: Both executives will focus on identifying and building new consumer brands, with Honasa signalling a clear strategic push beyond its existing portfolio

items.summary: Q4 Results Today, 13th May 2026 Live Updates: Catch all the live updates here from the companies announcing their financial results today

items.title: Gold import duty hike in focus: Are gold ETFs poised to gain traction after this move?
items.summary: The recent import duty hike on gold and silver has led to a surge in gold ETFs, which are expected to benefit from increased flows as investors seek diversification. However, the demand for physical gold may decline due to cultural and savings-driven reasons.

items.title: 'Bond markets on edge' as King Charles sets out fragile UK government's agenda
items.summary: U.K. gilts and bonds faced heavy selling pressure in response to the latest 'Starmer drama', as the prime minister faced down rivals ahead of the King's Speech.

items.title: Markets snap 3-day losing streak; metals rally as rupee hits fresh low
items.summary: The Sensex closed 49.74 points, or 0.07 per cent, higher at 74,608.98, while the Nifty 50 gained 33.05 points, or 0.14 per cent, to settle at 23,412.60 after a volatile trading session

items.title: Jamie Dimon warns JPMorgan may rethink new London office if 'very smart' Starmer is ousted as UK PM
items.summary: Starmer's leadership is under pressure, sparking concern in the bond market.

items.title: Delhi HC asks Google, Apple to act against obscene content on mobile apps
items.summary: The Delhi High Court has ordered Google and Apple to take strict action against obscene pornographic content on their mobile applications. The court emphasized the vital role of social media intermediaries in preventing the dissemination of such material. The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team has also been asked to check the spread of this content.

items.title: Gold volume growth may dip 10–15% after duty hike, say jewellers
items.summary: India's gold and silver import duty hike may affect jewellery sales volumes initially. Industry leaders believe demand will stay strong due to gold's cultural and investment importance. Consumers might opt for lighter jewellery. The government aims to conserve foreign exchange and boost domestic gold recycling. Initiatives are underway to encourage circulation of idle gold.

items.title: Bharti Airtel Q4 Results: Net profit rises 10.5% QoQ to ₹7,325 crore. Here are key highlights
items.summary: Bharti Airtel reported a Q4FY26 net profit of ₹7,325 crore, up 10.5% QoQ, with revenues at ₹55,383 crore, a 15.7% increase. India business revenue reached ₹39,566 crore, while ARPU rose to ₹257. The Homes business saw a 37.3% revenue growth.

items.title: Withdrawal of retirement funds soon to be automated: EPFO
items.summary: The Employees Provident Fund Organisation, EPFO, will soon automate final provident fund withdrawal claims. This will speed up money transfers to applicants. EPFO will also automate provident fund account transfers when a member changes employers. This means no more forms will be needed for account migration.

items.title: PM's call to cut foreign travel: Industry executives seek measures to boost inbound tourism
items.summary: Following PM Modi's call to postpone non-essential foreign travel, travel industry executives see an opportunity to boost 'Incredible India' marketing and attract more overseas tourists. Experts suggest increasing marketing budgets and simplifying visa processes, like e-visas, to enhance inbound tourism and earn foreign exchange, especially as international travel faces disruptions.

items.title: Tui sees summer sales fall 10% due to cautious UK customers
items.summary: The travel operator says customers are delaying booking holidays over Iran war concerns.

items.title: Bharti Airtel Q4 Results: Profit falls 34% YoY to Rs 7,325 crore even as revenue jumps 16%
items.summary: Bharti Airtel Q4 Results: Bharti Airtel reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 7,325 crore for Q4 FY26, a 34% year-on-year decline, despite a 16% revenue growth to Rs 55,383 crore. The company recommended a final dividend of Rs 24 per share and highlighted strong performance in India and Africa, alongside strategic investments in digital lending and AI.

items.title: Higher gold duty rattles bullion market; buyers cut purchases, opt for exchanges
items.summary: Rally in domestic gold prices following the duty increase, coupled with Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s appeal to curb non-essential gold imports, has triggered caution across bullion markets and jewellery showrooms

items.summary: Dixon Tech delivered a second consecutive quarter of 2% revenue growth in Q4FY26, hurt by weak smartphone demand, missed volume targets, and narrowing margins.

items.title: PMS Tracker: Money Grow Asset, Green Portfolio, 12 others post up to 44% returns in April
items.summary: A range of quantitative, thematic, multi-cap and small-cap PMS strategies posted strong gains in April, with top performers delivering returns of up to 44.39% amid a sharp equity market rebound. Broader markets led the rally, while debt-focused strategies largely underperformed or delivered muted gains.

items.summary: The fundraising includes 50 billion rupees of public debt and around 30 billion rupees of loans from a group of lenders led by State Bank of India, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information is private.

items.title: Stock Market Highlights 13 May 2026: Sensex, Nifty snap 4-day losing streak, end in green amid global headwinds, FII outflows
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Indian markets ended in green on Wednesday afternoon after a volatile session, with Sensex and Nifty posting marginal gains as sentiment remained fragile amid crude oil surge, foreign investor outflows, rupee weakness and persistent global inflation concerns.

items.title: Russia to fulfil all agreements on energy supply to India: Russian FM Lavrov
items.summary: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov has assured India that energy supply agreements will be completed. He stated that relations between Russia and India are based on deep friendship. Lavrov praised Prime Minister Narendra Modi as an energetic leader. He pledged that unfair competition will not harm their energy deals.

items.title: Tata Motors (CV) Q4 Results: Standalone profit spikes 70% YoY to Rs 2,406 crore; revenue jumps 22%
items.summary: Tata Motors CV Q4 Results: Tata Motors (CV) achieved a strong financial performance. Net profit surged 70% year-on-year to Rs 2,406 crore in the March quarter. Revenue also saw a healthy 22% increase, reaching Rs 24,452 crore. The company's board recommended a final dividend of Rs 4 per equity share. Full-year revenue stood at Rs 77,400 crore.

items.title: Cabinet clears upgradation of Nagpur International Airport
items.summary: Nagpur International Airport is set for a major upgrade through a public-private partnership. The Union Cabinet has approved extending the land lease for MIHAN India Ltd. This move allows GMR Nagpur International Airport Ltd to operate and modernize the airport. Passengers and cargo services are expected to see significant improvements.

items.title: A short squeeze or sentiment rally? Here's why SAIL shares surged 14% today
items.summary: Shares of Steel Authority of India (SAIL) jumped 14% on Wednesday, driven largely by a short squeeze as traders with heavy bearish derivative positions were forced to cover shorts. Extremely high MWPL utilisation and concentrated bets among a few clients amplified the rally as stop-loss triggers accelerated buying pressure.

items.title: Bayer’s FarmRise app reaches 5 million users
items.summary: Its phygital ecosystem for farmers is led by FarmRise, a farmer-focused digital platform

items.title: Cipla Q4 profit drops 55% to Rs 555 crore; board recommends Rs 13 dividend
items.summary: Cipla's fourth-quarter net profit saw a significant drop of 55 percent year-on-year. For the full financial year, the company's profit also declined. Despite these figures, key therapies and trade generics business showed strong growth. The company plans to focus on expanding its markets and developing its future pipeline. A final dividend of Rs 13 per equity share has been recommended.

items.title: Blackstone-backed Knowledge Realty Trust raises Rs 500 cr through issue of commercial papers
items.summary: Knowledge Realty Trust (KRT), India's largest REIT, has successfully raised Rs 500 crore through commercial paper issuance. This private placement will fund KRT's expansion plans. The 91-day commercial papers, set to mature on August 11, 2026, will be listed on the BSE's wholesale debt market.

items.title: Indian rupee hits record low as outflows, oil strain worsen rout
items.summary: The energy shock from the prolonged U.S.-Iran war ‌that has effectively ⁠shut ⁠the Strait of Hormuz has hurt India's macroeconomic outlook and strained its current account balance. Economists have ​lowered their growth forecasts, raised inflation projections and warned of sustained pressure on the rupee.

items.title: Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu to announce measures to counter West Asia war impact
items.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu will unveil precautionary measures within two days to counter the West Asia conflict's impact. The government will announce steps to safeguard the state from the US-Iran war's effects. He urged citizens to support economic strengthening by avoiding unnecessary gold buying and foreign travel.

items.summary: The board also recommended a bonus issue of 1:3, i.e. one additional equity share of Rs 10 each, fully paid-up for every three equity shares held by shareholders as on the record date, subject to the approval of the members of the company.

items.summary: Gaurav Kapoor Ventures has secured an exclusive mandate to manage sales for The Ruby, a 1 million sq.ft. LEED certified, Grade-A commercial tower in central Mumbai. This consolidated approach aims to provide a unified market position and curated buyer engagement for the prominent development, offering sea views and the Bandra-Worli Sea Link vista.

items.summary: Sri Lotus Developers and Realty Ltd reported an 11% increase in its consolidated net profit to Rs 95.57 crore for the quarter ended March. Total income for the fourth quarter surged to Rs 322.03 crore. For the full fiscal year 2025-26, the company's net profit grew to Rs 237.08 crore, with total income rising to Rs 818.71 crore.

items.title: Gold import duty hike in US may have led to insider trading, says Zerodha’s Nithin Kamath
items.summary: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath highlighted the tight regulation in Indian financial markets following a surprise import duty hike on gold and silver. He noted the absence of unusual trading activity before the announcement, contrasting it with potential scenarios in Western markets where privileged information might be exploited.

items.summary: Sectorally, Morgan Stanley is betting on domestic cyclicals over defensive and external-facing sectors, as key risks to India are mostly external, including geopolitical tensions and slowing global growth, it observed.

items.summary: After four sessions of declines, Indian markets rebounded on May 13, led by gains in metal stocks. The Nifty 50 and Sensex rose slightly, while broader indices outperformed. Government tariff hikes on precious metals and rising tensions in the Middle East influenced market dynamics.

items.summary: After four days of heavy selling, technical analyst Rohit Srivastava of Strike Money Analytics says proprietary sentiment indicators are flashing an oversold signal — a classic precursor to a meaningful recovery.

items.title: Cabinet raises Minimum Support Prices for 14 kharif crops
items.summary: The Union Cabinet has approved significant hikes in Minimum Support Prices for 14 Kharif crops for the 2026-27 marketing season. Sunflower Seed sees the largest absolute increase. Cotton and other oilseeds also receive substantial boosts. MSPs for all notified Kharif crops are set at least 50% above production costs. Common paddy MSP is fixed at Rs 2,441 per quintal.

items.title: China's Tencent sees boost from gaming, AI demand even as revenue comes in weaker than expected
items.summary: The tech giant reported its first-quarter 2026 earnings on Wednesday.

items.title: Dalcore launches North India’s first YOO-branded residences ‘The Falcon’ in Gurugram
items.summary: Dalcore has launched The Falcon, North India's first YOO-branded ultra-luxury residences on Gurugram's Golf Course Road. Designed by YOO Inspired by Starck and architected by UHA, the project offers 96 expansive 3 and 4 BHK homes across two acres. It aims to redefine design-led living with a focus on curated experiences, wellness, and sustainability.

items.title: No suspicious trades before gold duty hike? Zerodha's Nithin Kamath says that’s rare globally
items.summary: In a recent analysis, Kamath observed the normalization of insider trading globally, contrasting it with India's financial oversight. He emphasized the lack of unusual trading before the government's import duty increase on gold and silver.

items.title: India set to scale back Russian oil imports if US waiver lapses
items.summary: Iran war oil impact: Amidst the ongoing geopolitical turmoil, Indian oil refiners face a critical moment as the expiration of a US waiver looms this weekend. With the war affecting supplies from the Persian Gulf, India has lately seen a surge in Russian oil imports.

items.title: Carrier Global to minimise price increases in India, CEO says
items.summary: Carrier Global is working to keep prices stable in India amidst global supply issues. The company is investing $100 million in Andhra Pradesh for a new facility. This expansion aims to meet growing demand both in India and for exports. Carrier anticipates strong double-digit growth in India for the next decade.

items.title: Do you know why companies pay dividends? Key facts investors should know
items.summary: Dividends are a way for companies to distribute excess cash to shareholders, often indicating financial strength and stable earnings. They can enhance investor demand, influence capital allocation, and are typically associated with mature companies with steady cash flows.

Economists weigh in on the impacts of rising oil prices and economic stability, leaving the market in suspense about future monetary policy.

items.title: India lacks adequate scrap supply, only 25% steel demand can be met through scrap-based production: Former Steel Secy
items.summary: India's steel sector grapples with limited scrap for green production. Only 25 percent of steel demand is met by scrap-based manufacturing. Deteriorating iron ore quality further complicates efforts. The nation is exploring green steel through pilot projects and government missions. A Carbon Credit Trading System is expected to drive emission reductions. India's crude steel output is growing significantly.

items.title: Charli XCX joins Nothing as shareholder and global brand ambassador
items.summary: Consumer tech brand Nothing has named British pop star Charli XCX its first Global Brand Ambassador and a shareholder. This collaboration signifies Nothing's move to become a cultural brand, not just a hardware maker.

items.title: Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman bets on premium push, wider drinking 'repertoire'
items.summary: As Indian consumers diversify their drinking habits, Brown-Forman, the maker of Jack Daniel's, is poised for growth in the premium spirits market. The American company plans to bring in more products, driven by changing preferences and a desire for unique experiences.

items.title: Alzheimer's a key part of Eli Lilly's India specialty medicine push, says company's country head Winselow Tucker
items.summary: Eli Lilly has introduced Lormalzi in India, a pioneering treatment for Alzheimer's that specifically targets amyloid plaques, a significant contributor to the condition. Unlike previous therapies that merely alleviate symptoms, this innovative medication aims to modify the disease itself.

items.title: Rupee slips to its lowest-ever intraday level of 95.80 against the US dollar
items.summary: The Indian rupee hit a record low of 95.71 against the US dollar due to high energy prices and overseas debt repayments. Despite tariff hikes on precious metals, economists warn of sustained pressure on the currency amid rising inflation and geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Adani Group is jumping into Europe's deep waters. What can happen
items.summary: Adani Ports is making a significant leap beyond traditional port operations, venturing into Europe's complex offshore and subsea market. Through a new partnership with Oceaneering International and its Astro Offshore arm, the company aims to become a global maritime infrastructure and offshore-services platform, focusing on deepwater engineering and underwater infrastructure for energy projects.

items.title: WazirX launches crypto Futures with lowest trading fees and no volume threshold
items.summary: WazirX has launched crypto Futures trading for Indian traders, offering the lowest maker and taker fees in the market without volume thresholds. This move aims to democratize derivatives trading by allowing direct INR access and prioritizing user education on responsible practices. The initiative also supports WazirX's ongoing restructuring efforts by generating profits for creditor recoveries.

items.title: Gold price today May 13: Gold rates rise in Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Kolkata, Ahmedabad and Bengaluru
items.summary: 22K & 24K gold price today, May 13, 2026 across Indian cities

items.title: Rice University eyes deeper India push, weighs campus presence under NEP
items.summary: Rice University is looking at expanding its presence in India. This includes potential research and academic partnerships under the new National Education Policy. The US institution sees strong demand from Indian students. Rice is exploring all options for a physical presence in India.

items.title: PFC Q4 Results: Profit rises 24% to Rs 6,325 crore as interest income grows
items.summary: Power Finance Corp reported a strong rise in fourth-quarter and full-year earnings for FY26, supported by higher interest income, improved fee income and a reversal in impairment provisions. Lower credit costs and steady growth in core lending operations helped the state-run power sector financier deliver improved profitability despite higher finance costs.

items.title: Indian regulators oppose Pernod Ricard's liquor plea, firm says business 'hopelessly fettered'
items.summary: Indian regulators are opposing Pernod Ricard's latest court attempt to regain its Delhi liquor license. The French company's license was rejected due to serious allegations of collusion with retailers to boost market share. Pernod denies wrongdoing, but the ban has impacted its business for three years. The Delhi government cites public interest and regulatory risk in its opposition.

items.title: Pine Labs shares crash 20% as six-month IPO lock-in ends; down 31% from issue price
items.summary: Pine Labs shares dropped 20% to ₹151 on May 13 as a six-month lock-in for pre-IPO investors ended, allowing 92.4 crore shares to trade. The stock has declined 31.6% from its IPO price of ₹221.

items.title: Dividend alert! HPCL announces Rs 19.25/share dividend, its highest in five years. Check record date
items.summary: HPCL has declared a final dividend of Rs 19.25 per share, its highest payout in five years, with August 14 set as the record date. Shares gained 4% after the announcement, even as the stock remains down in 2026. Q4 profit rose 46% to Rs 4,902 crore.

items.title: Stellantis’ Leapmotor partnership points to a wider — and riskier— industry gamble
items.summary: A recent tie-up between Jeep maker Stellantis and China's Leapmotor is seen as a watershed moment for the future of European carmaking.
